explanation: Whole intact biological form with minimal processing — whole fruit
Sliced
notes: or chopped pieces. Shape altered but physical form not transformed.

explanation: Whole cereal or pulse grain. Distinct from fresh pieces — grain implies dry harvested seed.
notes: Added; EMF puts grain in N-axis not M-axis but needed here for raw agricultural materials.

explanation: Fibrous plant mass retaining cell structure. Not fully smooth — press residue or coarse mash.
notes: EMF combined pulp and puree; split here because they are legally distinct in India.

explanation: Smooth pourable plant-origin liquid. Cell structure fully broken. No discernible pieces.
notes: Legal and GST distinction from paste (paste is not pourable).

explanation: Smooth non-pourable spreadable form. Higher solids content than puree. Does not flow freely.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Distinct tax and legal treatment from puree in India.

explanation: Pressed or expressed aqueous liquid from plant material. Distinct from liquid — source is mechanical extraction.
General aqueous liquid not produced by pressing. Covers liquid milk
column6: Added; EMF does not have a generic liquid state. Juice is pressing-specific; liquid covers the rest.

explanation: Liquid with water significantly reduced by evaporation or membrane separation. Still pourable.
Stable liquid-in-liquid dispersion. One phase dispersed in another (e.g. butter
column6: Added; not in EMF. Important for dairy fat forms and some additive forms.

explanation: Set water-continuous matrix that holds shape at rest. Deforms under significant force.
Deforms readily under light pressure but is not liquid. Not gel-set. Covers fresh curd and soft unset forms.
column5: labne-style products that EMF does not distinguish.

explanation: Formed discrete particles larger than powder
emf_m_tag: smaller than grits. Has defined shape per particle.

explanation: Fine dry particulate. Non-grain origin or where grain origin is not the relevant characteristic.
column5: emulsifier powder) — those are not matter-states; source lives in the graph.

explanation: Fine dry particulate specifically from milled grain. Use this only for grain-origin fine powder.
notes: Narrowed from EMF's combined tag. Starch flour and whole wheat flour both map here; source differentiates in the graph.

explanation: Coarse dry particulate. Larger than flour
Thin flat solid sheets produced by rolling. Oat flakes
Pressed or defatted coarse solid remaining after oil or fat extraction from seed.
notes: Dropped the skim/defatted qualifier — that is process context
column5: not a distinct state. Meal = the residual solid mass.

explanation: Rigid pressed solid that holds shape without refrigeration. Paneer
Fixed lipid liquid at room temperature. Groundnut oil
Solid or semi-solid lipid at room temperature. Butter
column7: Distinct from oil — physical state at room temp differs.

explanation: Volatile aromatic oil fraction obtained by steam distillation or cold expression from plant material.
notes: Legally and functionally distinct from fixed oil.

explanation: Soluble fraction separated from source in liquid form. Covers aqueous and ethanolic extracts.
notes: EMF combined extract and oleoresin; split here because they are physically and legally distinct.

explanation: Viscous resinous extract containing both fixed oil and volatile aromatic compounds.
Crystalline solid form. Covers sugars
column6: Renamed — 'chemical' was EMF scoring language; not needed in declaration context.

explanation: Particulate material dispersed in liquid but not emulsified. Settles on standing.
